source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/06_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/독일 BfarM 등록/D. 기술문서 최신본/6. Product Verification and Validation/3. Cybersecurity/6. A-SBOM-HNX_SBOM(Rev.4).pdf

document.title: Cybersecurity Risk Management - Software Bill of Materials
document.documentType: Technical Documentation / Software Bill of Materials

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 4 SBOM and cybersecurity lifecycle document for the HnX-P1 and HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports cybersecurity risk management, software bill of materials, support lifecycle, and vulnerability assessment evidence for the Portable X-ray System.
keyPoints: Document No. A-SBOM-HNX, Rev. No. 4, Rev. Date December 10, 2025.
Product name is Portable X-ray System; model names are HnX-P1 and HnX-PB; manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The document states cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling are provided for Portable X-ray System(HnX-P1, HnX-PB).
HnX-PB is described as a variant model with only exterior design changed compared to HnX-P1, with interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, and other items the same.
HnX_P1 and HnX_PB software does not have a specific EOL/EOS; end date is determined by software changes or the product life cycle of 7 years.
Device firmware HnX_P1.hex and HnX_PB.hex underwent threat modeling, penetration testing, and static code analysis.
COTS and open-source components were cross-checked against NIST NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog, with no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting the product identified to date.

entities.name: HnX-PB
entities.evidence: The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the main model HnX-P1

entities.name: CISA KEV Catalog
entities.evidence: cross-checked against NIST's NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation Document No. A-SBOM-HNX, Rev. No. 4.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-SBOM-HNX Rev. No. 4

evidence.claim: The document title is Cybersecurity Risk Management - Software Bill of Materials.
evidence.evidenceText: Cybersecurity Risk Management - Software Bill of Materials

evidence.claim: The product covered is the Portable X-ray System, model HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Portable X-ray System Model name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The document aims to provide cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling for HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This document aims to provide cybersecurity lifecycle risk analysis and threat modeling for Portable X-ray System(HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The device includes an LCD display, X-ray generator, X-ray tube assembly, and collimator.
evidence.evidenceText: This portable X-ray system (model: HnX-P1, HnX-PB) consists of an LCD display with up and down soft keys for kV control, an X-ray generator (power transformer), an X-ray tube assembly, and a collimator.

evidence.claim: The system control software controls circuit modules within the X-ray generator in real time and responds to user input from the control panel.
evidence.evidenceText: The system control software interacts with and controls the various circuit modules within the X-ray generator in real time. This software responds to user input from the control panel.

evidence.claim: The product is intended for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with mobile or fixed locations.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with mobile or fixed locations.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB variant only changes exterior design compared with HnX-P1; interface, material, internal structure, PCB, and software are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the main model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional cybersecurity risk management is performed for adding HnX-PB because of the stated equivalence to HnX-P1.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional cybersecurity risk management is performed due to the addition of the variant model (HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The SBOM lists HnX_P1.hex and HnX_PB.hex as actively maintained application firmware by H&abyz, version 00.12, timestamp 25-05-04.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX_P1, HnX_PB HnX_P1.hex HnX_PB.hex H&abyz actively maintained 00.12 25-05-04 Until the product life cycle H&abyz Application

evidence.claim: Most listed STMicroelectronics software components are actively maintained hardware driver or system/start-up components with EOS until product life cycle, while the end of support date is unknown.
evidence.evidenceText: STMicroelectronics actively maintained 1.00 24-05-03 Until the product life cycle (The end of support date is unknown.)

evidence.claim: The KISA kisa_sha256.c component is listed as an actively maintained hash code generator.
evidence.evidenceText: kisa_sha256.c KISA actively maintained 1.00 25-02-18 Until the product life cycle (The end of support date is unknown.) KISA Hash code generator

evidence.claim: HnX_P1 and HnX_PB software does not have a specific EOL/EOS; the end date is determined by software changes or the 7-year product life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX_P1 and HnX_PB software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product. The end date is determined by software changes or the product life cycle (7 years).
evidence.pageTarget: End of Life and End of Support

evidence.claim: HnX_P1.hex and HnX_PB.hex are device firmware and were assessed through threat modeling, penetration testing, and static code analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: 'HnX_P1.hex' and 'HnX_PB.hex' are the device firmware; threat modeling was conducted for each function, and both penetration testing and static code analysis were performed.

evidence.claim: COTS and open-source components were cross-checked against NIST NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog.
evidence.evidenceText: all COTS and open-source components were cross-checked against NIST's NVD and the CISA KEV Catalog

evidence.claim: No publicly known vulnerabilities affecting the product were identified to date in the stated checks.
evidence.evidenceText: no publicly known vulnerabilities affecting this product were identified to date.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the document text; source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic but the source path references German BfArM registration.
No standards are explicitly cited in the source text; NIST NVD and CISA KEV Catalog are vulnerability resources, not standards in this document.
Several SBOM table rows state the end of support date is unknown, despite lifecycle language of until product life cycle.
SBOM table extraction has minor layout/OCR artifacts around page 6, including a stray leading '0' before an end-of-support note.
